red_lines: Any challenge to absolute personal authority
Territorial concessions or retreat from conquered lands
Threats to racial ideology and Lebensraum objectives
core_narratives: Lebensraum and eastward expansion for the German Volk
Racial supremacy and elimination of perceived racial enemies
Germany as master of a new European order

decision_history.event_description: Decision to invade the Soviet Union despite ongoing war with Britain
decision_history.decision_taken: Launched Operation Barbarossa with 3.8 million troops along a 2,900 km front
decision_history.outcome: Initial rapid advances but failed to capture Moscow before winter; war became attritional

decision_history.event_description: Japan attacked Pearl Harbor, bringing the United States into the war
decision_history.decision_taken: Declared war on the United States despite no treaty obligation to do so
decision_history.outcome: Created a two-front global war against the world's largest industrial power
context_factors: belief war with US was inevitable

decision_history.event_description: Soviet counteroffensive surrounded German 6th Army at Stalingrad
decision_history.decision_taken: Refused permission for breakout or retreat, demanded the army fight to the last man
decision_history.outcome: Complete destruction of 6th Army; 91,000 captured; turning point of the Eastern Front

cultural_context.domestic_power_base: Nazi Party apparatus, SS, Gestapo, propaganda machine, industrialists, Wehrmacht officer corps (diminishing loyalty)

military_posture.force_disposition: multi-front offensive war with strategic overextension increasing from 1942

recent_events: Allied bombing of German cities intensifying
